matches.rationale: Both are Bash rules governing git commits in the release pipeline, and the extracted evidence preserves the requirements to split changes into logical units and use Conventional Commits.
The Bash trigger is gh pr create and the evidence requires creating the PR from the current branch into main, exactly the gold action.

The rule's name and evidence require checking status with gh run watch after launching the Release workflow. Its regex is narrower and fires on the launch command rather than gh run watch itself, but it targets the same launch-follow-up action described by gold.
The Bash rule blocks gh pr merge and quotes the exact prohibition against merging immediately after PR creation without first checking CI.
Both are Edit rules triggered by a manual local version change to package.json. The extraction also mentions the later sync command, but its Edit target matches package.json as the file to change.

misses.note: No extracted Bash rule targets gh pr checks --watch or the distinct act of waiting for release-PR CI.
The extracted merge guard was assigned to the closer g11 wording and does not preserve this rule's specific CI-passed prerequisite plus squash-merge action as a separate 1:1 rule.

The only rule whose regex mentions gh workflow run Release is semantically labeled and evidenced as the post-launch gh run watch rule and is used for g7; no separate rule preserves the bump-selected workflow launch.
The conditional prohibition on merging when CI fails was not extracted as its own rule; the sole merge rule is reserved for the closer immediate-merge-without-CI prohibition in g11.
Compiler-reported skip: "이미 main 브랜치에 있고 변경사항이 없으면 릴리즈만 실행 — 조건부 워크플로우 분기 로직으로 단일 tool 트리거로 표현 불가".
No extracted Bash rule captures the ordered post-PR procedure of waiting for CI Tests or manually cancelling them before merge.
No extracted Bash rule targets the forbidden single-line command-chain structure gh pr create && gh pr merge.
Compiler-reported skip: "Release 트리거는 workflow_dispatch만 사용되고 main push로는 릴리스되지 않는다는 설명 — 시스템 동작 설명일 뿐 에이전트 행동 규칙이 아님".
The document expresses the prohibition indirectly as local manual version changes being unnecessary because the workflow handles them; no Edit rule captures that workflow-context prohibition.
The sync command appears only inside an Edit rule's evidence. Because gold requires a Bash follow-up after the package.json edit and tools must match, it cannot match that extracted rule.
The recommendation to choose workflow-based automatic version management was not extracted as a Bash action rule.
